participants.name: 한양대학교 (2y5skq)
participants.professional_persona: 한양대학교 건축공학과 교수로 혁신인재양성사업 교육연구팀장입니다. 스마트 건축 및 도시 재생 분야의 융합 인재 양성에 관심이 많습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 건축 환경 공학, 연구팀 예산 자율 편성 및 집행, 컨설팅 보고서 작성 및 평가 대응 노하우.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구팀이 자율적으로 예산을 효율적으로 활용하여 연구 성과를 극대화하고, 팀원들에게 안정적인 연구 장학금을 지급하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 광주광역시
participants.professional_persona: 광주광역시 소재 대학에서 기계공학 석사과정을 밟고 있으며, 3D 프린팅과 적층 제조 공정 최적화 연구를 하고 있습니다. BK21 사업을 통해 연구 장비와 재료비 지원을 받아 실험에 매진하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: CAD 설계 및 유한요소해석(FEA)에 능숙하고, 다양한 금속·고분자 재료의 적층 제조 조건 최적화 경험이 있습니다. 3D 프린팅 장비 직접 운용 및 유지보수도 가능합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 국내 방산 또는 항공우주 기업에서 연구개발 엔지니어로 근무하며, 첨단 제조 기술을 국산화하는 데 이바지하고 싶습니다. 박사 과정까지 연계하여 깊이 있는 연구를 계속할 계획입니다.

participants.name: 충남
participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-ujqihktv
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-ujqihktv
participants.professional_persona: 충남 소재 사립대학에서 신소재공학 박사 과정을 밟고 있으며, BK21 미래인재양성사업의 교육연구단에 참여하여 나노소재 합성 연구를 수행 중입니다. 온라인 간담회에 참석하여 대학원생 처우 개선에 대한 의견을 적극적으로 제시합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 나노입자 합성, 전자현미경(SEM/TEM) 분석, X선 회절(XRD) 분석에 전문성을 갖추었으며, 연구 성과를 특허로 출원한 경험이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 박사 후 국내 재료 연구소에 취업하여 디스플레이 소재 분야의 실용화 연구를 선도하고, 나아가 연구소장의 자리에 오르는 것이 꿈입니다.

participants.name: 생명과학
participants.professional_persona: 생명과학 전공 박사 후 연구원으로, BK21 사업 지원을 받아 신경퇴행성 질환의 분자 메커니즘을 연구하고 있습니다. 월 300만 원 이상의 급여로 연구에 전념할 수 있게 되었습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 세포 배양, 단백질 분석, CRISPR 유전자 편집 기술에 능숙하며, 공동 연구 프로젝트 경험이 많습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 해외 우수 연구소에서 포스트닥을 마친 후, 국내에 복귀하여 독립적인 연구실을 운영하는 것이 꿈입니다.

participants.skills_and_expertise: 디지털 스토리텔링, 가상현실(VR) 콘텐츠 제작, 지역 문화 기획 및 운영에 능숙합니다. 또한 관련 공모전에서 수상한 경력이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 석사 졸업 후 지역 문화재단이나 지자체에 취업하여, 지역 문화유산을 활용한 관광 콘텐츠를 개발하고 지역 경제 활성화에 이바지하고 싶습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a graduate student in the BK21 innovation talent training project at a regional university in Gangwon Province, I see the total funding allocated to Hanyang University's BK21 program as part of the national budget. The policy context states the annual budget for education research group support is 408,080 million KRW (2022). For Hanyang, I estimate a proportional share based on its size and number of groups. The graduate scholarship slots are derived from the total budget and stipend levels: master's 700K, doctoral 1.3M, doctoral candidate 1M KRW per month. With about 12,600 graduate students in future talent and 6,400 in innovation talent nationally, Hanyang likely hosts around 1,500-2,000 students. Given the budget and stipend amounts, I estimate roughly 19,000 slots nationally, but for Hanyang specifically, I assume a smaller fraction. Postdoc positions are fewer due to higher salary minimums (3M KRW/month) and limited group budgets. I estimate about 300 postdoc positions nationally, with Hanyang having a modest share. My perspective as a regional student makes me cautious about overestimating Hanyang's allocation, as resources are concentrated in top universities.

For evaluation panels, I estimate 3 formal meetings (annual, mid-term, compliance) based on the policy's description of evaluation types, though the mid-term evaluation is planned for 2023, so in 2022 only the annual and compliance checks may occur; I'll stick with 3 as a conservative estimate. Consulting sessions are held for groups that request them; the policy context says 280 out of 578 groups wished to receive consulting, so I use that number. Compliance checks are executed on the 205 innovative talent training groups, as stated in the policy. I have not seen other posts at this phase, so I rely on my own reasoning from the policy context and my perspective as a regional student who is aware of the program's operations.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of an education research group at Hanyang University, I reason that the sustained investment in graduate talent development over a decade, as seen in the BK21 program, will gradually enhance national innovation capacity. The program's focus on training 19,000 graduate students and 300 postdocs, with strong outputs of 38,000 publications and 7,600 international conference presentations, builds a critical mass of skilled researchers. Over time, as these alumni progress in their careers, they foster a research culture of integrity, collaboration, and interdisciplinarity, which is reinforced by mandatory online education and evaluation feedback loops. The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through publications, webzines, and forums influences other institutions, raising the overall S&T ecosystem's performance. However, South Korea's global rank in S&T talent competitiveness is unlikely to jump to the top immediately; given the program's scale and the competitive global landscape, I estimate a rank of 12, reflecting gradual improvement from a current rank around 15-20. For high-impact publications, the program's emphasis on quality and international collaboration, combined with the 38,000 publications, suggests that about 1.5% of these could be top 1% cited, yielding 570 high-impact publications. With 19,000 graduate students and 300 postdocs (19,300 total researchers), this gives 570/19.3 ≈ 29.5 per 1000 researchers, but considering that not all researchers are full-time equivalent and the time window is 2021-2022, I adjust to 15 per 1000 researchers, reflecting the early stage of impact.
evidence: The BK21 program's sustained investment of 408,080 million KRW annually supports 19,000 graduate students and 300 postdocs, producing 38,000 publications and 7,600 international conference presentations, which builds a strong talent pipeline for national innovation.

As alumni progress in their careers, they promote a research culture of integrity and collaboration, reinforced by mandatory online education and evaluation feedback loops, which enhances the quality of research outputs over time.

Dissemination of outstanding group achievements through publications, webzines, and forums influences other institutions, raising the overall S&T ecosystem's performance, but global competitiveness takes time to improve, leading to a rank of 12.

High-impact publications are a lagging indicator; with 38,000 publications and a conservative 1.5% top-cited rate, about 570 high-impact papers are expected, but per 1000 researchers (19,300 total), this yields 29.5, adjusted downward to 15 due to the early impact window and researcher FTE considerations.
